policy_history.renewal_notes: Experience modifier increased from 1.04 to 1.18 due to 2023 claims activity. Forklift fleet expanded from 4 to 7 units.

claims_history.description: Forklift operator suffered a lower back injury while lifting a pallet manually after a forklift malfunction. No forklift operator certification was on file for this employee.
claims_history.broker_action: Claim paid. During claim review the carrier flagged the lack of a documented forklift certification program as a future audit risk.

claims_history.description: Warehouse associate slipped on a wet floor near the loading dock with no wet-floor signage present, resulting in a sprained ankle and 3 weeks of lost time.
claims_history.broker_action: Standard claim. Carrier noted this is the second slip-and-fall at this loading dock location in 18 months during the loss-run review.

risk_flags.description: No documented forklift operator certification or training program exists despite the fleet expanding to 7 units. The 2023 claim involved an uncertified operator. OSHA 1910.178(l) requires certification, creating exposure for both workers comp severity and potential penalties.
Experience modifier has trended up from 1.04 to 1.18 over two consecutive years. Two slip-and-fall incidents at the same loading dock location suggest an unaddressed facility hazard.
